MEET
  • Physical Process Models in MEET
  • Combustion Process
  • View page source

Combustion Process

Inputs

\(N\) - Number of gas species.

\(g_{s}\) - Fuel gas mass fraction for species \(s\), as a mass ratio, \(\frac{kg}{kg}\)

\(\gamma_{s}\) - Fuel gas mass fraction for species \(s\), in driver units, \(\frac{kg}{< driver >}\)

\(d_{s}\) - Destruction efficiency for gas species \(s\) during the combustion process, \(\frac{kg}{kg}\)

\(c_{s}\) - Mass of CO2 emitted for complete combustion of gas species \(s\) during the combustion process, \(\frac{kg}{kg}\)

\(\dot{B}\) - Driver value, in driver units of \(\frac{< driver >}{s}\), such as \(\frac{bbl}{s}\) or \(kW\) which is \(\frac{kJ}{s}\)

Note that

\[\sum_{s = 1}^{N}g_{s} = 1\]

Gas Combustion Calculations

Gas sent to combustion is given by a driver value, \(\dot{B}\), in driver units (e.g. \(\frac{bbl}{s}\)). The gas composition file translates this driver into the mass flow of the gas stream:

\[{\dot{m}}_{s} = \gamma_{s}\dot{B}\]

with units of \(\frac{kg}{s}\).

In this gas flow the fraction \(d_{s}\), the species-specific destruction efficiency, is converted to CO2 and \((1 - d_{s})\) is released in the exhaust of the combustion process, commonly known as combustion slip. The emission rate by gas species is:

\[E_{s} = \left( 1 - d_{s} \right){\dot{m}}_{s}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]
\[E_{CO_{2}} = {\dot{m}}_{CO_{2}} + \sum_{s = 1}^{N}{\dot{m}}_{s}c_{s}d_{s}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]

with units of \(\frac{kg}{s}\). The above calculation must be converted back to a new gas composition line for output by MEET, \(\gamma_{s}^{*}\), with a driver of \(\dot{B}\). Substituting the earlier equation for \({\dot{m}}_{s}\):

\[E_{s} = \left( 1 - d_{s} \right)\gamma_{s}\dot{B}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]
\[E_{CO_{2}} = \left\lbrack \gamma_{CO_{2}} + \sum_{s = 1}^{N}\gamma_{s}c_{s}d_{s} \right\rbrack\dot{B}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]

This produces a new mass emission unit \(E_{s}\) for all species. To compute a new gas composition, this needs to be normalized by dividing through by \(B\ \dot{}\):

\[\gamma_{s}^{*} = \frac{E_{s}}{\dot{B}}\]

The new gas composition is:

\[\gamma_{s}^{*} = \left( 1 - d_{s} \right)\gamma_{s}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]
\[\gamma_{CO_{2}}^{*} = \gamma_{CO_{2}} + \sum_{s = 1}^{N}\gamma_{s}c_{s}d_{s}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ s = 1\ldots N,\ s \neq CO_{2}\]

with units of \(\frac{kg}{< driver >}\)
